title: Save, Publish & Serve
description: Add schema persistence with version numbering, a Publish button that marks the schema as active, and a GET API endpoint that serves the published schema to consuming applications.
acceptanceCriteria: PUT /api/forms/:formId/fields persists the updated schema and increments the version number
Each save creates a new version entry in the in-memory store with version number and timestamp
POST /api/forms/:formId/publish marks the current version as 'published' and sets it as the active schema
Publish returns a success response with { formId, version, status: 'published', publishedAt }
GET /api/forms/:formId/schema returns the active published schema as JSON
GET /api/forms/:formId/schema returns 404 if no published version exists for the formId
Response includes metadata: formId, version, publishedAt, title
Publish button added to the form editor UI using govuk-frontend button component
After publishing, a GOV.UK success notification banner is shown
Form dashboard shows 'Published' status badge next to published forms
Backend unit tests cover: save with version increment, publish, serve published schema, 404 for missing

techStack.dataLayer: In-memory store with version history array per form

definitionOfDone: Can save edits, publish, and then GET /api/forms/:formId/schema returns the published schema
Version number increments correctly on each save
Published forms visible on dashboard with correct status
Code passes TypeScript strict mode with no errors
Evaluator must run and produce a passing evaluation-03.json before sprint is marked complete
